---
species: "Chondrus crispus"
accepted_code: "CHCR"
alternate_codes: []
diminutive: ""
alternate_diminutives:
common_name: "Irish moss"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
description: "Flat blade, flat thalli, fan-like, regular branching, dark purple/red, or yellow/greenish fronds, up to 15cm tall"

image: '/header_images/chondrus_crispus.jpg'
categories:
  - erect red algae
sampling_programs:
    - keen_quad
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
habitat:
  - intertidal
  - subtidal
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 80300922
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 145625
---

![](/header_images/chondrus_crispus.jpg)

Photo from Irene Novaczek via [WORMS](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=image&pic=127585&tid=145625)

**Other images:** 
<br/>

**Distinguishing features:** Red algae with fan-like dichotomous branching. Frond is mostly flat. Has a blue iridescence underwater and in some lighting.
<!-- size, gross morphology, a key identifier, zonation notes (intertidal or depth) -->
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** *Mastocarpus stellatus* (MAST), also known as false irish moss. *Chondrus* has a flat stipe/thalli, although the edges of the blades might be curly. See [here](https://mainenaturalhistory.org/chondrus-crispus-vs-mastocarpus-stellatus/) for the definitive guide.

<br/>

**Extended Description:** Can be found intertidally or in the shallow subtidal. When reproductive, it will have oval shaped bumps on the edges.
<!-- Extended description -->

**[WORMS page](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=145625)**
